seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: input+output: generalize the coverage domain from a grid/forest of cells to an arbitrary weighted road graph, attach a per-district build cost, and require distance-r coverage of a demand subset (weighted distance-r dominating set) so the min-cost covering is NP-hard and greedy vs. randomized local search diverge.
title: Proofed & Provisioned: Bakery Depot Coverage
strategies: reference baseline: build a depot in every shop district (each shop covers itself)
cost-effective greedy set cover: repeatedly build the depot with the best build-cost per newly-covered-shop ratio
GRASP randomized multi-restart with redundancy pruning: sample near-best cost-effective picks across seeded restarts and drop redundant depots, keeping the cheapest feasible covering
LP-relaxation rounding / swap-based local search over the distance-r coverage sets
